query_normalized: investigating the role of microglial morphology in age-related cognitive decline neuroscience
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) because it explicitly measures and discusses "morphometric features of the microglia" (microglial morphology) and their plasticity, which is the core mechanism the user is investigating, even though the study focuses on the prenatal brain and vagus nerve manipulation rather than age-related decline. It also meets criterion (e) by providing a canonical study on microglial morphology assessment methods in a relevant neurobiological model.
